title: 'Abusive relationship': Deve Gowda responds to Kharge's 'loved us, married Modiji' dig
content: Former Prime Minister HD Deve Gowda responded to Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge's remarks by calling his alliance with the Congress a "forced marriage" that ended in an "abusive relationship.

title: 'Finish off what's left in Iran?' Trump sends 'straight' message to 'non-responsive' allies
content: US President Donald Trump has sharply criticised allies for not backing Washington's military actions against Iran, questioning their commitment. He stated the US, as the world's most powerful nation, doesn't need assistance, despite earlier calls for help to secure the Strait of Hormuz. Many key allies have expressed reluctance to join offensive operations.

title: 'Okay if Gambhir wanted me, Kohli & Rohit out': Ashwin backs India coach's call to 'move on'
content: Ravichandran Ashwin explained his retirement decision, stating he wanted to leave on his own terms after feeling his time was up. He also backed head coach Gautam Gambhir, emphasizing that the coach has the right to make team-oriented decisions, even if it means senior players like himself, Kohli, or Rohit must move on.

title: PM Modi talks to Kuwait's Crown Prince; reiterates India's stand on regional peace
content: Prime Minister Modi conveyed Eid greetings to Kuwait's Crown Prince and condemned attacks on the nation's sovereignty amidst regional conflict. He emphasized India's priority on safe navigation through the Strait of Hormuz and discussed diplomatic solutions for peace. Similar concerns were raised with the UAE President, highlighting India's energy ties and diaspora welfare.

title: Nvidia’s Jensen Huang become first CEO to roll out token budgets for recruitment
content: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang proposed offering engineers annual "token budgets" as part of their compensation, potentially amounting to half their base salary. This move aims to boost engineer productivity and serve as a new recruiting tool in Silicon Valley. Huang believes this will amplify engineers' capabilities, making them significantly more effective.

title: What if Koneru Humpy skips Women’s Candidates? Fine, replacement, and 'setback for India'
content: Indian chess star Koneru Humpy is uncertain about playing the Women’s Candidates in Cyprus due to Middle East tensions. She recently voiced concerns publicly. However, a final decision is yet to be taken. Her withdrawal could lead to fines and impact Indian chess. If she opts out, Ukraine's Anna Muzychuk may replace her in the tournament.

title: ‘Felt humiliated and suffocated’: After joining BJP, Pradyut Bordoloi slams Congress
content: Nagaon MP Pradyut Bordoloi has joined the BJP, citing persistent internal humiliation and lack of leadership support within the Congress. He expressed deep hurt over allegations of a criminal nexus against a candidate being dismissed as "fabricated" by a Congress leader.

title: 5 IPL legends who have never won the Purple Cap
content: Several IPL greats like Sunil Narine, Piyush Chawla, Ravichandran Ashwin, Jasprit Bumrah, and Ravindra Jadeja have taken plenty of wickets but never won the Purple Cap. Their focus on economy, control, and team roles often limits big wicket hauls, despite consistent performances over many seasons.

title: Sam Altman sends 'thanks note' to engineers with the message that 'we plan to take your job'
content: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman expressed gratitude to early software engineers, acknowledging their complex work. However, his comments sparked debate about AI's impact on coding jobs, with users interpreting his message as a prelude to displacement. This follows recent workforce reductions and AI investment announcements from companies like Block Inc. and Atlassian, signaling a broader trend of automation affecting white-collar roles.

title: IPL 2026: Ishan Kishan to lead SRH, Abhishek Sharma also gets new role
content: Sunrisers Hyderabad have appointed Ishan Kishan as captain for the upcoming IPL season, with Abhishek Sharma as his deputy. This leadership change is in effect until regular captain Pat Cummins recovers from injury. Kishan, a dynamic wicketkeeper-batter, brings his explosive form and proven IPL record to the role, aiming to lead the franchise effectively during Cummins' absence.

title: Pentagon replies to Anthropic's lawsuit: Refusal to accept the govt's contractual terms is not protected
content: The US Justice Department has labelled AI firm Anthropic a national security risk, rejecting its free speech claims in a lawsuit. The Pentagon argues Anthropic's refusal to allow 'any lawful use' of its AI models poses a sabotage risk during combat. This dispute, stemming from contract disagreements over AI usage, could cost Anthropic billions, while the Pentagon explores alternatives.

title: 'We feel uncomfortable': Kangana Ranaut calls Rahul Gandhi 'tapori'
content: BJP MP Kangana Ranaut labeled Rahul Gandhi a "tapori," claiming women MPs feel uncomfortable around him due to his "rude" demeanor. This follows an open letter from retired bureaucrats and lawyers criticizing Gandhi's protest behavior as a "disregard for parliamentary authority" and "arrogance."

title: This child’s unusual drawing shocked her dad: Here’s what it teaches parents about kids’ minds
content: A father posted his child's whimsical drawing of him tumbling off a roof with a big grin, igniting lively discussions online. Art specialists point out that children's creative expressions often intertwine reality with imagination and emotion, seldom signaling distress. Parents are encouraged to engage in open dialogue with their kids, using these artworks as windows into their thoughts and feelings.

title: KKR's Cameron Green gets angry at reporter; interview ends with 'waste of time' remark
content: The incident took place outside Western Australia’s dressing room after the third day of the match on Monday. A report in The Age detailed the interaction with 'visibly annoyed' Green, stating that the interview began with a question about Green’s century for Western Australia against New South Wales.

title: Elias Pettersson hits 200th NHL goal in Canucks win over Panthers
content: Elias Pettersson had a milestone game, as he scored twice to hit his 200th goal in the NHL and enable the Vancouver Canucks to defeat the Florida Panthers in a decisive manner. His maturing legacy came into the limelight and helped in an eventual NHL match outcome in Vancouver.

title: Punjab to seek water royalty from Rajasthan; Mann claims Rs 1.44 lakh cr dues since 1960
content: Punjab's Chief Minister Bhagwant Mann has demanded royalty from Rajasthan for water supplied since 1960, citing a 1920 tripartite agreement. He claims Rajasthan owes Rs 1.44 lakh crore under this pact, which facilitated water flow through the Rajasthan feeder. Mann stated Punjab has formally written to Rajasthan and plans to involve the central government to resolve the unpaid dues.

title: Raghav Chadha proposes joint tax filing for married couples — here’s how it could work
content: Aam Aadmi Party MP Raghav Chadha proposed reforms to ease financial burdens, advocating for optional joint income tax filing for couples, full tax exemption on disability pensions for all wounded soldiers, and removal of bank minimum balance penalties. These proposals aim to create a fairer financial system for ordinary citizens amidst ongoing pensioner concerns.

title: 'Anxiety ho rahi thi': Shivam Dube explains why he took train after T20 World Cup win
content: Following India's T20 World Cup 2026 victory, Shivam Dube bypassed his flight to rush home to his family. He opted for an early morning train to Mumbai, traveling discreetly in a 3rd AC compartment. Dube's crucial contributions with bat and ball cemented his status as an impactful all-rounder in the triumphant campaign.

title: Helicopter overturns and crashes in eastern Nepal while landing, one injured—Watch
content: An Air Dynasty helicopter carrying a dead body and five passengers crashed while attempting to land in a field in Nepal's Khotang district. One passenger sustained injuries, while the pilot and other passengers were unharmed. High winds or dust during landing are suspected as contributing factors to the incident.

title: Bank holidays in March: How many days are banks closed this week? Check state-wise list
content: March 2026 brings a series of bank holidays across India, impacting branch operations significantly. Customers should note closures for festivals like Gudhi Padwa, Ugadi, Eid-Ul-Fitr, and Ramzan, alongside state-specific observances and the second and fourth Saturdays. Digital banking remains available for routine transactions, but services requiring branch visits must be rescheduled.

title: Home at last: SC Delhi bring ISL to the capital after seven long years
content: Sporting Club Delhi, formerly Hyderabad FC, will end the capital's seven-year absence from first-division football as they host Jamshedpur FC at the Jawaharlal Nehru Stadium on March 19. Despite a rocky start to the season with one draw and three losses, the club is banking on home support and a passionate fanbase to spark a turnaround.

title: India opens anti-dumping probe into ethyl chloroformate imports from China
content: India has launched an anti-dumping probe into Chinese ethyl chloroformate imports following a complaint by domestic producer Paushak, alleging unfair pricing that harms local industry. The investigation, initiated by the Directorate General of Trade Remedies, will assess if duties are needed to protect Indian manufacturers. This move is part of a broader effort to curb cheap imports.

title: ‘Favourites tag doesn’t come easy’: Shubman Gill’s bold Team India message
content: Shubman Gill, recipient of the Polly Umrigar Award, emphasised the collective effort behind India's dominant status and their hard-earned reputation as favourites. He highlighted the team's fearless approach, shaped by coach Gautam Gambhir and support staff, and reflected on a successful past year, looking forward to crucial upcoming Test series.

title: Indore: Video captures sparks after blast in EV charging point that triggered short circuits
content: A devastating fire, originating from an EV charging point, rapidly engulfed a three-storey house in Indore, exacerbated by multiple LPG cylinder explosions. The blaze claimed seven lives and injured three, prompting investigations into EV safety and storage of flammable materials. Authorities are establishing an SOP for EV charging.

title: Risk vs roti: Indians stay back in Gulf as war intensifies - here's why
content: Amidst escalating Middle Eastern tensions, many Indian blue-collar workers are staying put for jobs and family support, despite recruiters warning of potential layoffs. While salaries remain consistent, some workers are on mandatory leave as demand for labour declines. Experts foresee a hiring surge post-conflict, but current uncertainty impacts operations and domestic hiring.

title: Internet reveals why Vaibhav Sooryavanshi shouldn't have watched 'Dhurandhar'
content: 14-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi went viral after naming Dhurandhar his favourite film at the BCCI Naman Awards 2026, sparking debate as it’s A-rated. While excelling in cricket with record performances, he aims to win IPL 2026 with Rajasthan Royals. Balancing fame and studies, he also missed a Class 10 board exam due to commitments.

title: 'Will be very bad for Pakistan': Afghan IPL star warns after attack, seeks India's help
content: Afghanistan spinner Allah Ghazanfar made an emotional plea to the world after a devastating airstrike hit a Kabul rehabilitation centre. He highlighted the human cost of escalating Pakistan-Afghanistan tensions, urging global intervention to de-escalate the conflict. Ghazanfar stressed India's close ties and the need for dialogue to prevent such tragedies.

title: ‘Freedom of speech cannot be absolute’: Vaishnaw on Nora Fatehi’s ‘Sarke Chunariya’ ban
content: The Union government has officially banned the song 'Sarke Chunariya' following widespread outrage over its vulgar lyrics and visuals. Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw confirmed the ban in Lok Sabha, emphasizing that freedom of speech in India has reasonable constitutional limitations concerning public order, decency, and morality. The move comes after numerous complaints and criticism from women's commissions and public figures.

title: 'Kind people': Iran thanks India as it receives first medical aid shipment from New Delhi
content: India has dispatched its first medical aid shipment to Iran, a gesture met with gratitude from the Iranian Embassy. This assistance arrives as Iran faces significant casualties and attacks on healthcare facilities due to ongoing military campaigns. Despite challenges, Iran's healthcare system remains largely functional, with contingency plans in place for worsening conditions and potential health risks from environmental factors.

title: Assam MP Pradyut Bordoloi joins BJP; CM hints at recommending him for polls
content: Assam MP Pradyut Bordoloi has switched from Congress to BJP, a day after leaving his former party. Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma welcomed him, suggesting Bordoloi will contest assembly elections. Bordoloi cited internal party issues and feeling insulted. This move precedes the upcoming Assam Assembly elections, with the BJP seeking a third term.

title: India buys more Russian crude oil amid US-Iran war; tankers headed to China make U-turn
content: Data from Vortexa Ltd. indicates that at least seven vessels transporting Russian crude have altered their routes mid-journey, changing course from China to India. At present, all major Indian refiners are actively sourcing Russian oil.

title: We reviewed the first week of NFL free agency: Additions, subtractions and questions for all 32 teams
content: Let's break down the main free agency signings, latest buzz on moves and biggest remaining questions for every team.

title: Live tracking NFL free agency: Updates on signings, trades, cuts
content: We're following NFL free agency signings, trades, cuts, contract restructures and other moves as they happen.

title: WNBA, union reach verbal agreement on new CBA
content: After a prolonged labor battle, the WNBA and players' union have reached a verbal agreement on the terms for a new CBA.

title: Sources: Dolphins star Achane off-limits in trade
content: The Dolphins have informed inquiring teams that De'Von Achane is not available in a trade, sources told ESPN.

title: Trucks driver parked for mocking IndyCar racer
content: NASCAR driver Daniel Dye has been suspended -- the second known major penalty of his racing career -- for mocking IndyCar driver David Malukas during a recent livestream.

title: Mark saves day, sends Texas to NCAA first round
content: Tramon Mark hit a fadeaway jumper from just inside the 3-point line with 1.1 seconds left, and Texas survived a late rally to beat NC State 68-66 on Tuesday night in a First Four matchup.

title: Other shoe drops: Caruso T'd up for desperate D
content: Thunder guard Alex Caruso was called for goaltending and a technical foul for an illegal defensive move after he swatted away a layup attempt with his shoe.

title: Tiger 'working,' but Masters status still unclear
content: Tiger Woods remained mum on his playing status for next month's Masters, telling reporters after the TGL semifinals that his health continues to be up and down.

title: U.S. laments another WBC loss as star offense lags
content: Team USA, boasting one of the most talented rosters ever assembled, failed to live up to its billing Tuesday night and could only watch as Venezuela celebrated its first World Baseball Classic championship.

title: Ex-reality TV star Holmes repeats Iditarod win
content: Former reality TV star Jessie Holmes cruised to a victory in the Iditarod, becoming the third competitor in the 54-year history of the race to repeat the year after winning for the first time.

title: 🏀 Giant Killers: Identifying men's tourney upsets
content: Which double-digit seeds have the highest potential to advance to the second round? Here's what ESPN's BPI says.

content: From mid-major leaders to national superstars, here's who could impact their teams' NCAA tournament runs.

title: 2026 men's NCAA tournament guide: How far will every team go?
content: ESPN's bracketologist shares what he really thinks about every team's chances in the NCAA tournament.

title: 15 Beers, booming NIL sales, not a sip of alcohol: The irony of Raegan Beers' viral jersey
content: Beers has never had alcohol, but she seized the opportunity to capitalize on her name when her jersey went viral.

title: Ranking the 25 best players in women's March Madness
content: Who are the top players in the women's NCAA bracket? Where do Azzi Fudd, Hannah Hidalgo and Lauren Betts rank?

title: The 10 best freshman and sophomore guards in women's March Madness
content: One must-see guard scores from anywhere like Kelsey Plum. Another handles pressure like Chelsea Gray.

title: Kiper's new NFL mock draft: How free agency shook up first-round landing spots for top prospects
content: Team needs have shifted in free agency, so how will top prospects come off the board in Round 1? Mel Kiper Jr. projects the NFL draft's first 32 picks.

content: Our NBA Insiders answer the biggest questions facing contenders in the East and West as the regular season winds to a close.

content: From rookies, veterans and Sixth Man of the Year candidates, Insiders break down this season's unsung heroes.

title: Coaches predict the men's NCAA tournament champion, potential Cinderellas, more
content: There was one clear favorite among the 25 coaches polled. Here are their takes on seven contenders and more.

title: 60 ESPN personalities pick their men's Final Four, national champions
content: Sixteen teams are in the mix for our semifinal predictions, with one emerging as the clear title favorite.

title: 20 contenders, 20 lingering weaknesses: Barnwell fixes roster holes as free agency slows down
content: Will Aaron Rodgers check the QB box for the Steelers? Can the Seahawks find running back help? And is there something the Chiefs can do at cornerback?

title: Fantasy WNBA: How to play fantasy women's basketball
content: Are you ready for another exciting season of Fantasy Women's Basketball? André Snellings walks you through the scoring system, roster sizes and other key details.

title: Champions League's most epic comebacks: Sporting join Barcelona, Liverpool among biggest revivals
content: Sporting became only the fifth club in Champions League history to win a two-legged knockout tie despite losing the first leg by three goals or more.

title: PSG show Chelsea what it takes to be truly part of Europe's elite
content: Tuesday's 3-0 defeat to PSG in the Champions League showed just how far off the pace the Blues really are.

title: As Premier League sides crumble around them, Arsenal keep cruising closer to a quadruple
content: Arsenal should feel the pressure as Premier League leaders. Instead, they look comfortable as a quadruple is in their sights.

title: NHL playoff watch: Will the Penguins catch the Hurricanes?
content: Pittsburgh faces off against Carolina on Wednesday. Plus, full playoff projections, draft lottery standings after a busy Tuesday.

title: Men's tournament: Best bets for the round of 64, plus Eliminator picks
content: As you prepare for tipoff, here are our best bets for the first round, plus picks for the new Eliminator game.

title: Jackson Chourio, Roman Anthony among must-draft players in fantasy baseball
content: Which players are Tristan H. Cockcroft higher on than most? Here's a list of 20 names to keep in mind when drafting your 2026 rosters.

title: In final window before World Cup, Pochettino's USMNT roster shows softened stance on club form
content: Mauricio Pochettino once demanded peak club performance from his USMNT players, but his March squad raises the question of whether that's negotiable.

title: Transfer rumors, news: Liverpool, Man United want Wolves' João Gomes
content: Wolves midfielder João Gomes is a target for Liverpool and Manchester United. Join us for the latest transfer news and rumors from around the globe.

title: 'I salute every boxer out there' -- Lamar Jackson boxes in offseason workout
content: Jackson is looking to bounce back after a hamstring injury which saw him play in only 13 games last season.

title: Women's boxing pound-for-pound rankings: Gabriela Fundora's dominance fuels her rise
content: Gabriela Fundora delivers another KO to break into the top three. Here are the latest women's pound-for-pound rankings.

title: PM condoles deaths in fire incidents in Indore, Delhi; announces ex gratia
content: PM Modi said that for the fire incidents in Indore and Palam, Delhi, an ex-gratia of ₹2 lakh from PMNRF would be given to the next of kin of each deceased; the injured would be given ₹50,000

title: Dredging in limbo at Beypore port amid delay in EIA
content: The harbour development initiative, launched three years ago under a ₹11.8-crore contract awarded by the Kerala Maritime Board, had been suspended after dredgers encountered hard laterite rock on the seabed

title: Action will be taken after legal opinion on ‘waqf’ RTC entries without documents: Revenue Minister
content: Krishna Byre Gowda says several properties have been recorded as waqf in Record of Rights, Tenancy and Crops without backing documents such as sale deeds, gift deeds, registration papers or grant orders; these include 21.21 acres of government land and 3,903.11 acres of private land in Bengaluru.

title: 99.35% of Sakala services delivered within deadline, auto-mutation covers 98%: Krishna Byre Gowda
content: The Revenue Minister says reforms introduced over the past two-and-a-half years had streamlined services at Nada Kacheris and tahsildar offices.

title: Commercial advertisement hoardings to be banned near heritage structures
content: Urban Development Minister Byrathi Suresh says that over the past seven to eight years, thousands of unauthorised advertisements had come up across the State without payment of any fee or tax to local bodies, resulting in significant revenue losses running into hundreds of crores.

title: Rare hailstorm lashes Shivamogga
content: Within minutes, the sound of rain gave way to the rattle of ice pebbles striking metal rooftops and windshields

title: CPI slams govt. over move to weaken Higher Education Council
content: Diluting its authority can open the door for increased control by private and corporate institutions and allow substandard institutions to operate, warns party State secretary Eswaraiah

title: Tamil Nadu poll | How a government contract cost a Congress MLA his election: a lesson for 2026 candidates
content: The victory of P. Veldurai, the Congress candidate, was overturned five years later, not because of voter fraud or malpractice at the booth, but due to a legal disqualification

title: Fresh debate over GTD’s future in JD(S)
content: G.T. Deve Gowda says he will consult the people of the Chamundeshwari constituency if the party rejects his application for a ticket

title: BMTC to roll out smart ticketing system with 11,000 digital ETM devices
content: The long-awaited upgrade will replace existing fare collection methods with smart electronic ticketing machines (ETMs), aimed at improving efficiency and commuter convenience, according to BMTC officials

title: How delayed civic polls choked Bengaluru’s air quality?
content: Civic polls in Bengaluru were last held in 2015, and councillors’ terms ended in 2020. As a result, the BBMP missed out on securing ₹1,475.90 crore under the 15th Finance Commission’s million-plus cities challenge, which could have been used to address the rising levels of PM10) in the city.

title: Minister urged to address ‘medicine shortage’ in government hospitals, PHCs
content: Patients are forced to purchase even basic medicines from private pharmacies, says Praja Arogya Vedika

title: Uncleared waste dumped at street corners, vacant sites posing health hazards in Yadgir
content: Though civic staff collect garbage from households, often residents, commercial establishments and vendors dump it in vacant sites, street corners

title: Amaravati N-9 roadway blooms with Tecoma flowers
content: The initiative is being spearheaded by Amaravati Development Corporation, which planted these shrubs along the central medians of a nearly two-kilometre stretch from Seed Access Junction to the Secretariat

title: JD(S) leader alleges that revenue officials hand-in-glove with land mafia in Hassan
content: The MLA also alleged that the district administration had failed to act on the illegal mining activities in the government at Mallapanahalli

title: 3,100 Telegram channels, 800 websites disabled by govt for hosting pirated contents
content: Cinematograph Act has strengthened the legal framework to deter film piracy with sections 6AA and 6AB, which prohibit unauthorised recording and transmission of films, Minister said

title: Govt. sanctions ₹119 crore for building Telugu Cultural Centre in Amaravati
content: It is expected to emerge as a landmark institution in the capital region, aimed at preserving, promoting and showcasing the State’s rich linguistic and cultural heritage

title: ₹260 crore sought to develop 50 slums as part of golden jubilee: Zameer Ahmed
content: Replying to Congress member K. Govindaraj in the Legislative Council on Wednesday (March 18), the Housing Minister said the Chief Minister has assured that funds will be provided in the supplementary Budget.

title: CII Mysuru Zone unveils new leadership team
content: Under the new team, CII Mysuru Zone is expected to further intensify its efforts towards fostering innovation

title: Karnataka transport department extends deadline for sleeper bus safety compliance to March 31
content: Operators have been directed to follow NHRC road safety guidelines, which outline eight compulsory safety measures.

title: Preeti Kamakar elected Mayor and Hanumant Kongali Deputy Mayor for Belagavi
content: Both are members of the BJP, that enjoys a majority in the House. There are 35 BJP members in the 58-member House.

title: Massive fire in Sector 22 market in Chandigarh
content: An official said the presence of some chemicals in one of the shops increased the intensity of the blaze

title: Gold worth ₹2.5 crore seized in the Nilgiris
content: The jewellery was brought to the Nilgiris to be supplied to private jewellery stores and was seized after the company officials failed to submit necessary documents

title: This national-level Paralympic swimmer dons many hats
content: Geetha Kannan, a national-level Paralympic swimming medallist with gold and silver medals to her name, recently became a junior cooperative auditor at the Directorate of Cooperative Auditing

title: Govt approves ₹6,969 crore for four-lane access-controlled road project in U.P. to boost connectivity
content: The project would generate 36.54 lakhs person-days (direct) and 43.04 lakh person-days (indirect) new employment opportunities

title: Kerala Assembly Polls 2026: V.D. Satheesan alleges Model Code of Conduct violations, asks EC to take down government hoardings
content: Satheesan seeks action against people responsible for violating the Model Code of Conduct (MCC) and demands strong monitoring mechanisms to prevent further breaches of the MCC

title: West Bengal Assembly elections: BJP ‘trapped’ Mamata in her seat by nominating Suvendu from Bhabanipur, says Dilip Ghosh
content: BJP leader Dilip Ghosh said that the move has removed the elbow room the Chief Minister usually keeps to cover her bases across West Bengal during her poll campaigns for the Trinamool Congress

title: Cabinet approves ₹33,660 crore BHAVYA scheme to accelerate industrial development
content: The scheme aims to develop world-class industrial infrastructure, unlocking manufacturing potential and driving India's growth story, Information and Broadcasting Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw said while briefing reporters on Cabinet decisions

title: DMK MP moves Madras High Court seeking direction to ED to register cases against AIADMK leaders
content: Rajya Sabha member R. Girirajan wants Enforcement Case Information Reports to be registered against S.P. Velumani, C. Vijayabaskar, M.R. Vijayabaskar, P. Thangamani, R. Kamaraj, K.P. Anbalagan, K.C. Veeramani, and two others

title: Kolkata I-PAC raids case: ED not a ‘person’ to claim violation of fundamental rights by a State, Bengal argues in SC
content: The Supreme Court asks the West Bengal government if the ED is then expected to just 'look and watch' as the Chief Minister barges in and obstructs I-PAC raids, with no remedy in court

title: ‘Dhurandhar’ merchandise case: Madras High Court prohibits unauthorised sale on Amazon, Flipkart, and Instagram
content: Justice Senthilkumar Ramamoorthy also directs a host of Internet Service Providers to block pirated versions of the sequel Dhurandhar The Revenge on websites

title: Three held for illegal LPG refilling racket in Narsingi; large haul seized
content: During the inspection, officials found that domestic cylinders were being illegally refilled into commercial cylinders

title: Man shot dead outside gym in Chandigarh, second firing in 24 hours
content: The attackers came on a motorcycle, and one of them opened fire at the victim, identified as Charanpreet, as he was stepping out of a gym, they added.

title: Tamil Nadu Assembly election: How to check your name in electoral roll before March 26 deadline
content: If your name is not yet included and you wish to vote in the April 23 election, you can apply for inclusion until March 26

title: Indian vessel ‘Jag Laadki’ carrying over 80,000 metric tonnes of crude oil reaches Gujarat’s Mundra Port
content: Adani Ports, which operates Mundra Port, said in a statement that the crude oil carried by ‘Jag Laadki’ was sourced from the UAE and loaded at Fujairah Port there

title: ECI reshuffles 13 IAS officers in West Bengal, appoints DM-cum-DEOs ahead of Assembly polls
content: According to an official communication issued by the poll body, officers including Jitin Yadav (Cooch Behar), Sandeep Ghosh (Jalpaiguri), Vivek Kumar (Uttar Dinajpur), Rajanvir Singh Kapur (Malda) and R. Arjun (Murshidabad) have been posted as DM-cum-DEOs

title: Watch: Eight killed in Indore house fire after EV blast; Govt. orders probe
content: Eight members of a family, including two children, were killed in a fire triggered by an explosion at an electric vehicle charging point in Indore early on Wednesday, March 18.

title: Tamil Nadu election: Persons with disabilities to launch campaign for accessibility in Chennai
content: Disability Rights Alliance member Smitha Sadasivan said, “We want more demonstrations on EVMs so that persons with disabilities and their supporters can get a clear idea of how to plan the voting process in a manner suitable to each individual.”

title: Telangana Police constitute nine-member SIT to probe Moinabad drug, arms case
content: An official memorandum issued by the Director General of Police on March 17 stated that the nine-member SIT will function under the overall supervision of Future City Commissioner of Police G. Sudheer Babu.

title: Assam study sheds new light on sun’s surface tremors
content: The tiny five-minute vibrations may hold big answers for scientists trying to understand the powerful solar storms that sometimes affect Earth, Tezpur University researchers say

title: Prajwal Revanna sexual abuse case: SIT files charge-sheet against 39 for circulating explicit content
content: The accused allegedly purchased 70 pendrives to circulate the obscene content, and the police reportedly found that Harish, one of the accused, purchased them by paying through a UPI payment. The police collected the details of the purchase.

title: Tamil Nadu election: EPIC valid across State if voter is on electoral roll, says ECI
content: Even if a voter presents an EPIC issued in another constituency anywhere in the State, it will be accepted as proof of identity, so long as their name is listed in the electoral roll of the concerned polling station

title: Tenure of parliamentary panel on simultaneous polls extended
content: The extension allows the committee to gather more insights from constitutional experts and political leaders as it deliberates on key legislation aimed at synchronising national and State elections

title: India's semiconductor market to reach $300 billion by 2035: Deloitte report
content: “India is poised for a structural shift as local production is expected to meet more than 60% of domestic demand by the end of 2035,” a Deloitte report said

title: Bengaluru CCB arrests key gun supplier from Madhya Pradesh
content: Police recovered three pistols, a dismantled revolver, and around 50 rounds of ammunition from the accused. In total, multiple firearms and over 50 live bullets have been seized in the case so far.

title: Centre taking action against coaching centres for misleading advertisements: Pralhad Joshi
content: Issues of misleading advertisements by coaching centres have come down relatively, Mr. Joshi said, adding that many measures have been taken against violation of consumer protection rights

title: Tungabhadra Left Bank Canal breach leads to water loss in Koppal
content: A breach in the Tungabhadra Left Bank Canal, located between Basapur and Bandiharlapur villages in Koppal taluk, resulted in the loss of large quantities of water late on Tuesday night.

title: Pradyut Bordoloi joins BJP after quitting Congress ahead of Assam polls
content: This has put a question mark on the fate of Mr. Bordoloi’s son, Prateek Bordoloi, who is the Congress candidate from the Margherita Assembly seat in eastern Assam

title: In politics, there's no full stop: PM bids farewell to 59 Rajya Sabha members; hails Deve Gowda, Kharge, Sharad Pawar
content: Addressing the Upper House on the occasion of the biennial farewell, PM Modi said such moments naturally dissolve partisan divides

title: 2016 teachers' assault case: Court rejects discharge plea of Mumbai Mayor Ritu Tawde
content: According to the prosecution, the conflict erupted over the "sudden transfer" of a lady teacher who was reportedly battling cancer.

title: Maharashtra government takes action against absentee doctors
content: Public Health and Family Welfare Minister Prakash Abitkar informed the Maharashtra Legislative Council that the government has initiated steps to improve transparency and efficiency in the health department

title: SSLC annual exam-1 begins across Karnataka, over 8.65 lakh students enrolled
content: As many as 4,44,172 boys and 4,21,816 girls from 15,941 high schools for the examination across the state.

title: Acid attack on minor in Maharashtra village; locals demand arrest of accused
content: According to police, the girl was heading home from the school in the afternoon when she was attacked. She is currently taking treatment at the hospital in Loni

title: Kerala Assembly polls: LDF ally RJD announces candidates; Bhaskaran, P.K. Praveen, and Anil Kumar to contest
content: Rashtriya Janata Dal to field M.K. Bhaskaran from Vadakara in Kozhikode, P.K. Praveen from Koothuparamba in Kannur, and P.K. Anil Kumar from Kalpetta in Wayanad as LDF candidates

title: Hyderabad sees intermittent showers after midnight rain, IMD forecasts more thundershowers
content: At 10.49 a.m., IMD issued an alert forecasting light to moderate thundershowers for the next few hours in the northern parts of the Greater Hyderabad Municipal Corporation (GHMC).

title: Government mandates airlines to reserve 60% of seats free of charge
content: The Ministry, in its latest release, urges strict adherence to ‘passenger rights framework, particularly in cases of delays, cancellations and denied boarding’

title: Parliament Budget Session LIVE: Both Houses adjourned for the day
content: While Lok Sabha discussed on the demand for grants under the control of the Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare, Rajya Sabha saw PM Modi bid farewell to 59 members

title: Watch: Why is Maharashtra’s Freedom of Religion Bill so controversial?
content: Maharashtra’s Freedom of Religion Bill, 2026 has sparked a major political and legal debate. The proposed law aims to prevent forced religious conversions through strict rules, including a 60-day prior notice and severe penalties. While the government says it protects vulnerable individuals, critics argue it could violate fundamental rights, enable misuse, and impact personal freedom and interfaith relationships. The bill has been passed in both houses.

title: Three MBBS students killed in scooter accident in Kerala’s Kozhikode
content: The accident took place around 2.30 a.m. when the electric scooter they were travelling on crashed into an electric post and fell into a drainage

title: Eight killed in Indore house fire after EV charging point blast; probe ordered
content: There was an explosion at the charging point, and the fire in the car then engulfed the house, says Police Commissioner Santosh Kumar Singh

title: LPG crunch pushes PM POSHAN kitchens back to firewood, burdening women workers
content: The PM POSHAN scheme which serves about 11 crore children across 10.35 lakh schools, mostly from socially disadvantaged and low-income backgrounds, is now reverting to alternative fuels

title: BJP-NCP candidates secure top posts in Latur Zilla Parishad
content: The Zilla Parishad elections were held on February 7 and appointment of the president concluded on March 16

title: A kidney sale that led to a CB-CID probe in Tamil Nadu
content: The nexus of agents with doctors and hospitals in the illegal sale of kidneys was exposed, the then Deputy Superintendent of Police V. Parthasarathy, who led the Organised Crime Unit of the CB-CID in investigating the case, wrote in a police journal published in 2010

title: The fires of Madras’ kitchens
content: Amid the burning issue of LPG shortage, here’s a look at how cooking fuel has evolved over the years in Madras

title: Work to construct boat jetties in Edapalayam irrigation tank near Tiruvannamalai town commences
content: Funded under the Special Grant Fund 2025-26, the boating facility will be built at a cost of ₹2.62 crore

title: Election officials seize ₹6 lakh unaccounted cash in Vellore, nearby districts
content: In a similar incident, election officials seized ₹2.5 lakh from textile traders near Ambur town in Tirupattur when they stopped their vehicle on Chennai - Bengaluru Highway (NH 48)

title: Man Kills Brother Over "Inappropriate Gaze" At Wife In UP's Mathura
content: A man allegedly hacked his younger brother to death with a sharp-edged weapon for "casting an inappropriate gaze at his wife", police said on Wednesday.

title: Not Just IT, Other Ministries May Soon Get Powers On Social Media Blocking
content: An inter-ministerial team is working on a proposal to allow multiple government ministries to issue blocking orders to social media platforms for fake or harmful content.

title: Iran's Stance On Nuclear Weapon Development Is Unchanged Says Foreign Minister
content: Iran's stance against the development of nuclear weapons won't significantly change, Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi told Al Jazeera.

title: Chinese University Tells Students To 'Fall In Love' During Spring Break
content: A Chinese university is urging students to "enjoy the flowers, fall in love" during their mid-term break, an unusual directive in a nation obsessed with getting good grades, as authorities seek new ways to spur marriages and domestic consumption.

title: Larijani's Visit To Daughter's House Sealed His Fate. How He Was Located
content: Iran's security chief Ali Larijani's killing deals a major blow to Tehran's power structure as it retaliates to US-Israeli attacks amid a war that has pushed the Middle East into fresh chaos.

title: Israeli Journalist Gets Death Threats After Reporting Missile Strike
content: On Polymarket, a prediction market, users had wagered millions on whether a missile would hit Israel on March 10.

title: 2.6 Lakh People Returned To India From Middle East Since Feb 28: Foreign Ministry
content: Amid the escalating West Asia conflict, the government on Wednesday said around 2.6 lakh people have returned from the region to India since February 28.

title: 9 Killed, Including 3 Children, In Fire At Building In Delhi's Palam
content: Nine people, including three children, were killed after a fire broke out in a residential building in southwest Delhi's Palam area on Wednesday morning, officials said.

title: Chinese Woman Hospitalised After Being Startled By Humanoid Robot
content: A woman on an evening walk in Macau was briefly hospitalised after a humanoid robot startled her and left her feeling unwell, police told AFP on Wednesday.

title: Iraq Restarts Oil Exports Via Turkey Amid Strait Of Hormuz Disruptions
content: Iraq announced on Wednesday it had resumed limited oil exports of 250,000 bpd through the Turkish port of Ceyhan after the country's output plunged due to disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz.

title: To Escape House On Fire, Delhi Man Threw Toddler From 3rd Floor, Then Jumped
content: Nine members of a family, three of them children, died in today's fire tragedy at southwest Delhi's Palam. Businessman Rajender Kashyap lived in the building with his wife, five sons, a daughter, four daughters-in-law and seven grandchildren

title: Live-In Partner, A Lawyer, Helps Police Arrest Notorious Nagpur Gangster
content: Notorious criminal Raja Ghouse, wanted in several cases, was arrested on Wednesday in Maharashtra's Nagpur district.

title: Centre Urges States To Cut Local Levies In Push For PNG Adoption
content: The Union Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas has urged states and Union Territories to reduce local levies and procedural hurdles on City Gas Distribution (CGD) projects to improve the investment climate and accelerate the adoption of PNG.

title: Why The Larijani Family Was Called The Kennedys Of Iran
content: The five Larijani brothers, all bearded and bespectacled, spent nearly five decades consolidating their power.

title: China Accuses Australia And New Zealand Of "Colonial-Style Arrogance"
content: China condemned Australia and New Zealand on Wednesday for "colonial-style arrogance" and poor treatment of Indigenous people after a joint statement by the two nations raised concerns over Beijing's rights record.

title: Cabinet Approves Rs 2,584 Crore Small Hydro Power Development Scheme
content: The Union Cabinet on Wednesday approved the 'Small Hydro Power (SHP) Development Scheme' for the period FY 2026-27 to FY 2030-31 with a total outlay of Rs 2,584.60 crore.

title: Congress Names Four Cross-Voting MLAs In Haryana, Fifth Still A Secret
content: The Haryana Congress has announced the names of four of the five MLAs who cross-voted during yesterday's Rajya Sabha election. But the secrecy over the fifth name has intensified the row that started since the voting last evening.

title: Iran's Intelligence Minister Esmail Khatib Killed In Strikes: Israel
content: Israel's Defence Minister Israel Katz said on Wednesday that they "eliminated" Iran's Intelligence Minister Esmail Khatib in a strike overnight.

title: Senior AIUDF MLA Aminul Islam Quits, Likely To Join Asom Gana Parishad
content: Political analysts said the MLA's resignation will be a major setback for the party ahead of the Assembly elections.

title: Bomb Hoax At Gujarat Assembly Complex Minutes Before Session
content: The Gujarat Assembly complex in Gandhinagar received a bomb threat via email on Wednesday, which turned out to be a hoax after a thorough check of the premises.

title: ESPN's final points-league mock draft for 2026
